reasoning: The question asks if Virgin Galactic (SPCE) will file for bankruptcy before January 1, 2026, and the current knowledge cutoff is November 9, 2025. This leaves less than two months until the resolution date. According to the search summary, as of Q2 2025, SPCE had $508 million in cash. Given a projected quarterly cash burn of roughly $110 million, the company should maintain a cash balance of approximately $300 million as of January 1, 2026. Management and analyst projections widely expect their financial runway to last until at least late 2026 to coincide with the anticipated start of Delta-class commercial flights. We see no imminent debt maturity or major catalysts that would prompt a Chapter 11 filing in the brief seven-week window remaining before the resolution date. It is practically certain they will not file for bankruptcy before 2026. I am assigning the minimum permitted probability of 5%.
